prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.50) and high seasonality variance (0.41), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_DWT_catboost excels at seasonal capture. The true series exhibits mixed trends across folds (up-up-down), requiring models that adapt to changing patterns. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal as it leverages model diversity without over-relying on any single approach.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_DWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_catboost", "late": "ONLY_FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_rf", "rf", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["DWT_svr", "FT_svr", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4994,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.50) and high seasonality variance (0.41), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_DWT_catboost excels at seasonal capture. The true series exhibits mixed trends across folds (up-up-down), requiring models that adapt to changing patterns. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal as it leverages model diversity without over-relying on any single approach."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.4994 and seasonality_corr_variance=0.4149 favor DBA combination (recommended_method_hint). With n_windows=3 ≤ 4, small top_k=3 is appropriate with shrinkage=0.35 to prevent overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific winners while inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 provides robust weighted averaging."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: dba_combination (baseline), best_per_horizon_by_validation (selection), topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (selection), inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 (weighted). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). All candidates are safe from leakage (baselines use no fitting, weighted/selection use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3). No candidate exceeds 4 of same type. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation leads RMSE/MAPE but current set already has diverse coverage. Score margin top2 is minimal (0.0004) indicating competitive performance. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.50) and high seasonality variance (0.41), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_DWT_catboost excels at seasonal capture. The true series exhibits mixed trends across folds (up-up-down), requiring models that adapt to changing patterns. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is optimal as it leverages model diversity without over-relying on any single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates high model diversity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.4994 and seasonality_corr_variance=0.4149 favor DBA combination (recommended_method_hint). With n_windows=3 ≤ 4, small top_k=3 is appropriate with shrinkage=0.35 to prevent overfitting. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific winners while inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 provides robust weighted averaging.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has 4 candidates with diverse types: dba_combination (baseline), best_per_horizon_by_validation (selection), topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 (selection), inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35 (weighted). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). All candidates are safe from leakage (baselines use no fitting, weighted/selection use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3). No candidate exceeds 4 of same type. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation leads RMSE/MAPE but current set already has diverse coverage. Score margin top2 is minimal (0.0004) indicating competitive performance. No changes needed.
